title: Pick learning-rt orchestrator inference path (cheap thinker)
description: Learning runtime' own loop cannot use Anthropic OAuth (routes to empty extra_usage pool per issues #6475, #12905). Needs alternative. Options: Nous Portal free tier (designed for learning-rt), local Ollama/llama.cpp, OpenRouter free routes. Decision criteria: zero ongoing cost, latency tolerable for orchestration, JSON tool calling reliable.

title: Verify claude-code skill routes through Max subscription (no API spend)
description: Learning runtime claude-code skill delegates via 'claude -p' subprocess. Claude Code uses Max OAuth. End-to-end test: invoke claude-code skill from learning-rt, confirm zero API charges + work completes. Without this, whole architecture defaults to API billing - deal-breaker per user (gmecocci, Max x20).

title: Install learning-agent + Python 3.11 toolchain
description: System python is 3.9; learning-rt needs 3.11+. uv already installed. Run learning-rt installer per https://github.com/external-rt-ref/learning-agent. Verify ~/.learning-rt structure. Confirm CLI works standalone before integration.
close_reason: learning-agent v0.11.0 cloned to ~/.learning-rt/learning-agent, uv venv with Python 3.11.15, core deps installed, learning-rt CLI verified

title: EPIC: Rebuild specify on learning-agent (logic in learning-rt, TS = UI shell)
description: Migrate all agent logic to external-rt-ref/learning-agent. TS specify becomes thin client: webapp + CLI wrapper + spec.yaml editor only. All learning, agent loop, memory, skills move to learning-rt via skill pack. Direction approved 2026-04-27.

title: Fork worker pool for parallel verify concurrency
description: DONE in commit 407edab. Worker pool implemented (src/daemon/worker.ts, worker-pool.ts). Closing for audit.
close_reason: Shipped in commits 407edab (daemon worker pool, memory, inspector) + 243967f (webapp WIP)

title: qa-feedback skill: persist user corrections (Honcho + memories + observations)
description: New skill / learning-rt tool that ingests feedback events and writes to three layers: (1) Honcho dialectic memory for stable user preferences, (2) ~/.learning-rt/profiles/specify/memories/qa-patterns.md for discrete rules with why+how-to-apply, (3) specify.observations.yaml companion file for per-spec derived behaviors with provenance.

title: specify.observations.yaml schema + loader in spec-driven skill
description: Sibling file to specify.spec.yaml, user-derived behaviors with source provenance. Schema: areas/behaviors mirroring spec.yaml plus 'source', 'session', 'created_at', 'confidence' fields. spec-driven skill loads spec + observations together; observations treated as soft checks (lower stop-on-fail priority).

title: Design QA feedback channel: webapp -> learning-rt
description: Live QA session: webapp shows action timeline; user inline-flags events. Event schema: (session_id, behavior_id, event_id, feedback_text, kind in {missed_check, false_positive, important_pattern, ignore_pattern}). Transport via websocket bridge (SP-h99). Webapp UI: per-timeline-event flag affordance + free-text comment.

title: Confirm learning-rt concurrency model vs current worker pool
description: Worker pool (commit 407edab) runs multiple specify tasks in parallel. Confirm learning-rt supports profile-level or session-level parallelism matching this. If not, schedule above learning-rt (lightweight scheduler spawning N agent sessions).

title: Replace TS daemon (HTTP inbox) with learning-rt equivalent
description: Current daemon: long-running agent + HTTP inbox (commits 9501602, 7e07c20). Either use learning-rt messaging gateway or write small learning-rt plugin exposing HTTP inbox. Specify CLI shells into this.

title: Bridge: learning-rt events -> TS webapp WebSocket
description: Webapp needs live activity stream/timeline/narrative panel (commit 243967f). Learning runtime has session store + FTS5 but live event bus unconfirmed. Build learning-rt-side toolset/gateway that pushes events over WebSocket. Webapp subscribes.

title: Scaffold spec-driven skill pack (SKILL.md + tools)
description: Create skill pack at templates/learning-rt-skills/spec-driven/ following agentskills.io standard. SKILL.md frontmatter with name/description/version. Tool entries that read specify.spec.yaml, resolve templates, drive spec-driven workflow. Loaded into specify profile.
close_reason: skill pack scaffolded at templates/learning-rt-skills/spec-driven/ (SKILL.md + references + report template); symlinked into profile and visible via learning-rt skills list as source=local

title: Port specify.spec.yaml interpreter as learning-rt tool
description: Current TS spec interpreter (src/) reimplemented as Python tool registered via learning-rt tools/registry.py. OpenAI-schema params, JSON-string return. Reads spec.yaml, emits structured plan the agent loop can consume.

title: Create 'specify' learning-rt profile (RUNTIME_HOME)
description: Use learning-rt profile feature for isolated specify instance. RUNTIME_HOME=~/.learning-rt-specify or similar. Sets boundary so specify learning does not pollute default learning-rt profile.
close_reason: profile 'specify' created at ~/.learning-rt/profiles/specify with 81 bundled skills synced; sticky default set

title: UI: agent introspection (daemon inspector)
description: DONE in commit 407edab first cut: daemon GET / serves live inspector (src/daemon/inspector.ts). Webapp integration remains follow-up.
close_reason: Shipped in commits 407edab (daemon worker pool, memory, inspector) + 243967f (webapp WIP)

title: Learned memory system for verify agent
description: DONE in commit 407edab. JSON store at .specify/memory, memory_record + memory_list tools, playbook/quirk/observation rows scoped (spec, target). Closing for audit.
close_reason: Shipped in commits 407edab (daemon worker pool, memory, inspector) + 243967f (webapp WIP)

title: Zellij sidebar spinner: not working in sandbox, missing question-mark for blocked prompts
description:
## Problem

Two issues with the Zellij sidebar status indicator:

### 1. Spinner not visible in sandbox sessions
When running Claude inside a Docker sandbox (clauded/cll), the spinner never shows in the Zellij sidebar. Works (sometimes) for regular Claude sessions in a Zellij pane but never for sandboxed sessions.

### 2. No question-mark indicator when Claude is waiting for user input
Even in regular (non-sandbox) sessions within Zellij, there's no distinct indicator when Claude is blocked waiting for user input (permission prompt, question). This matters because the user can't tell at a glance that Claude stopped making progress and needs attention.

## Expected Behavior
- Spinner should work in both regular and sandbox Claude sessions
- A distinct indicator (e.g. ?) should appear when Claude is asking a question or waiting for permission

## Context
Status line and notification hooks are configured in functions.zsh and the Docker entrypoint (ensure-plugins.sh). The sandbox sets up notification hooks but spinner state may not propagate through the Docker boundary.

title: Self-evolution cron: scope, frequency, rollback path
description: learning-agent-self-evolution (DSPy + GEPA) regenerates spec-driven + qa-feedback skill prompts from accumulated session traces + feedback. Schedule via 'learning-rt cron'. Decisions: how often (weekly?), input corpus size, prompt diff review gate, version + rollback strategy. Versioned skill files in repo so reverts are git-trivial.

title: Active QA mode: same-session pattern propagation across siblings
description: When user flags a pattern on one element ('check empty state on this form'), agent immediately probes the same pattern across siblings (other forms in current session). Different from cross-session learning - happens live during the QA run. Skill prompt extension that watches feedback events and queues sibling-checks.

title: Confidence model: per-check acceptance rate -> autonomy threshold
description: Each verification check accumulates accept/override stats from user feedback. Threshold drives 'ask vs run silently vs report later' behavior. UI: slider preset for 'ask everything / ask uncertain / autonomous'. Defaults shift as feedback accumulates. Stored alongside qa-patterns.md or in skill state.

title: Delete TS agent loop, worker pool, learned memory
description: Once learning-rt path verified end-to-end, remove src/ agent loop, worker pool, learned memory store, message injection. Webapp + CLI shim + spec.yaml editor only remain on TS side.

title: Migrate webapp activity stream to learning-rt event source
description: Webapp components (activity stream, action timeline, narrative panel) re-pointed at learning-rt WebSocket bridge. Remove TS-side event producers.

title: CLI shim: 'specify' command -> learning-rt wrapper
description: Keep specify CLI as ergonomic entrypoint. Internally execs 'learning-rt --profile specify' with skill pack preloaded and spec.yaml passed as initial context. Preserves user muscle memory.

title: Migrate templates/ and scripts/ into skill assets
description: Move project-internal templates and scripts into the spec-driven skill pack as bundled resources. Preserve invocation paths from CLI standpoint.

title: Quirk triage: file-and-continue pattern
description: DONE in commit 407edab as part of learned-memory system. Agent records quirk rows with severity + suggested_fix; continues rather than blocking. Closing for audit.
close_reason: Shipped in commits 407edab (daemon worker pool, memory, inspector) + 243967f (webapp WIP)
